$comment: D-FFI-TCL-DESCRIPTOR (SQLite testfixture arc, C1 2026-07-17): the neutral <tcl.h> FFI descriptor. SQLite's `testfixture` (the binary that runs the 1190-file .test suite = 'all sqlite units') is ~90 dense C TUs linked against libtcl8.6, and every src/test*.c uses TCL's C API. testfixture calls TCL DIRECTLY — its build recipe (main.mk `make -n testfixture`) carries NO -DUSE_TCL_STUBS (that flag is only on the loadable-EXTENSION targets tclsqlite-{shell,stubs}.o), and /usr/include/tcl8.6/tcl.h has ZERO tclStubsPtr references — so these are plain extern prototypes, NOT the stubs-table macro form. That makes tcl.json an ORDINARY (if large) FFI descriptor, exactly like the libc ones. | LINKAGE: the elf `library` is libtcl8.6.so (TCL's SONAME — it ships an UNVERSIONED soname; `readelf -d libtcl8.6.so.0` -> SONAME libtcl8.6.so, and the runtime pkg libtcl8.6 ships BOTH libtcl8.6.so and the libtcl8.6.so.0 compat file, so the soname resolves without tcl8.6-dev). The ELF walker emits one DT_NEEDED per DISTINCT referenced import library (D-FFI-MATH-LIBM-DT-NEEDED, the libm.so.6 precedent), so a <tcl.h> user links libtcl8.6.so ALONGSIDE libc.so.6 — witnessed by examples/c-subset/shipped_tcl_eval (RUN -> exit 42 on elf x86_64). | availableObjectFormats:[elf] — TCL exists on pe/macho too but with different sonames + no run witness yet; a pe/macho <tcl.h> include fails loud until those legs land (later arc cycle). | OPAQUE HANDLES: Tcl_Interp/Tcl_Obj are modeled like stdio.json's FILE — a named struct with an opaque byte blob, used only via ptr<...> (never by-value), so member access fails loud and the blob size is inert. C1 shipped the eval surface (5 functions); C2 (2026-07-17) adds the COMMAND-REGISTRATION surface (Tcl_NewIntObj/Tcl_SetObjResult + Tcl_CreateObjCommand, whose 3rd param is a FUNCTION POINTER `ptr<fn(...)>` — the signal.json handler precedent) so a C command can be registered and CALLED BACK by libtcl — the core shape of every SQLite test*.c command (witnessed by examples/c-subset/shipped_tcl_command → 42). C4 (2026-07-17) adds the SCALAR/STRING/RESULT surface a real test TU uses: the ClientData typedef (void*), the TCL_OK/TCL_ERROR/TCL_STATIC/NULL PP-macros (the `macros` surface — signal.json's `#ifdef`-safe precedent), and Tcl_GetString/Tcl_WrongNumArgs/Tcl_SetResult/Tcl_AppendResult (the last VARIADIC, the stdio printf-family precedent) — witnessed by examples/c-subset/shipped_tcl_strcmd (a string-arg command → 42) and grounded by src/test_backup.c's error count dropping (34 → the next Tcl_CmdInfo-introspection layer). C5 (2026-07-17) adds the COMMAND-INTROSPECTION layer: the Tcl_CmdInfo struct (the FIRST field-access struct here — 8 fields, int + 7 pointers, modeled via the `structs` surface so `cmdInfo.deleteProc` resolves + libtcl fills it at the ABI-correct offsets) + Tcl_GetCommandInfo/Tcl_SetCommandInfo/Tcl_DeleteCommand/Tcl_GetIndexFromObjStruct — witnessed by examples/c-subset/shipped_tcl_cmdinfo (registers a command, introspects it via Tcl_GetCommandInfo, checks isNativeObjectProc==1 [a runtime layout witness] → 42) and grounded by test_backup.c reaching a CLEAN compile (0 errors). 16 functions total; the full 106-symbol test-TU surface + the real Tcl_Obj layout + CONST/Tcl_Size land as later cycles' real test-TU consumers demand them.
